newsSinceJun6: No new company-specific fundamentals since June 6. June 8's +6.5% was the broad semiconductor recovery (Huang 'buy at a discount' comments); June 9's -4.1% is the sector selloff extending (Broadcom's soft AI forecast June 5, hot May payrolls stoking rate fears) - flow-driven, not fundamental. The material catalysts (Investor Day 2030 plan: double revenue, >2x EPS, HBM4/HBM5 test intensity +25-30%, CPO as multi-hundred-million-dollar systems engine; Evercore upgrade to Outperform, $155 PT June 4; director Rhodes ~$0.8M insider sale June 2) all predate the window and were in yesterday's verdicts.

sources.label: GuruFocus - FORM shares surge 6.5% (Jun 8), Investor Day analyst attention
Investing.com - Why is FormFactor stock down ~6% (sector selloff, payrolls, Evercore $155 PT context)
Yahoo Finance - FormFactor targets revenue doubling by 2030 on AI chip testing boom (CPO, HBM5)
StockStory via FinancialContent - FormFactor, Entegris, Amtech soaring Jun 8 on sector recovery

verdicts.rankRationale: No new evidence since yesterday's hold; the rank has already improved to 11 post-Investor-Day and the 'smaller market visibility' premise is eroding as sell-side coverage (Evercore $155 PT) discovers the name. Today's -4.1% is sector beta, not a thesis event, so hold with mild drift down on residual-alpha grounds.
verdicts.bull: Test intensity is structurally rising 25-30% per HBM generation into HBM4/HBM5, and probe cards are consumable-like, giving recurring leverage to every AI memory and ASIC ramp.
verdicts.neutral: The 2030 double-revenue plan is credible but now public and priced into a +101% YTD, $9.3B name; near-term quarters must validate it.
verdicts.bear: Probe-card demand is cyclical and memory-weighted; a pause in HBM capacity adds or a broader AI-capex digestion (Broadcom-style guide-down) compresses both estimates and the rerated multiple.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 results/guide confirming HBM4 probe-card pull and CPO systems orders against Investor Day targets
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating multiple compression if HBM test-intensity orders pause or the AI semi selloff deepens

verdicts.rankRationale: The page-specific CPO thesis was explicitly validated by management - CPO named the 'engine' of doubling the systems segment by 2030, a multi-hundred-million-dollar opportunity - but that disclosure predates June 6 and was in yesterday's hold. Today's flow-driven -4.1% slightly improves entry on an intact thesis, so hold with small positive drift.
verdicts.bull: FormFactor is one of the few pure test/known-good-die plays on silicon photonics and CPO, where wafer-level optical probing is a genuine bottleneck nobody else owns at scale.
verdicts.neutral: CPO revenue is still small relative to the probe-card core; the multi-hundred-million 2030 target is a management aspiration, not booked backlog.
verdicts.bear: If CPO adoption timelines slip (as optical transitions historically do), the systems-segment growth engine stalls while the stock already prices the option.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Concrete CPO/silicon-photonics test design wins or systems-segment revenue acceleration in upcoming prints
verdicts.primaryRisk: CPO adoption slipping right as crowding builds post-Investor-Day rerating

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday already moved it down for the +101% YTD rerating with no hidden alpha left at rank 37; nothing new since June 6 justifies a second consecutive move, and today's -4.1% sector-beta dip neither damages the thesis nor restores enough asymmetry.
verdicts.bull: Upstream pick-and-shovel exposure to every advanced-node, HBM and chiplet ramp, with a management roadmap to double revenue by 2030.
verdicts.neutral: Real AI test leverage, but fully recognized: +101% YTD, fresh sell-side upgrades, and insider selling at the highs mark a discovered name.
verdicts.bear: A cyclical probe-card business trading on a 2030 narrative; estimate cuts in any memory/logic test digestion would hit a stretched multiple hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Execution proof points against the Investor Day 2030 framework in Q2/Q3 results
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclicality of probe-card orders versus a valuation that already embeds the AI test-intensity story
